behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: explicit image reaches publication. To catch this, the real vision model must actually attempt to classify the image—a mock will always return the "correct" classification and hide cases where the real model misclassifies, times out, or fails. Beyond classification, the queue/storage and publish-decision logic must also be tested, but those are dependencies of the core issue: the model must work.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post containing an explicit image to the moderation service using the real vision model. Verify it is stored in the review queue and does not appear in the published feed.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Real failure: obfuscated hate speech is not flagged by the real text model. A mock will always return a static decision; the real model's robustness to obfuscation is non-deterministic and must be validated against actual inference. Mocking hides the possibility that the real model is vulnerable to evasion techniques.
behaviors.test_description: Submit text posts with hate speech obfuscated using multiple techniques (letter substitution, unicode lookalikes, etc.) to the real text classifier. Verify all variants are flagged and held, not published.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: a safe post that should be published is held or lost. The core failure is in publish-decision logic or the publish system itself, not model correctness. We can test this with safe inputs (which we construct to intentionally pass both checks) and mocked models returning "pass", then verify the publish system actually distributes the content. Mocking the models is sufficient because the behavior is conditional on passing both checks; we are not testing model robustness here.
behaviors.test_description: Create a post with safe image and text content. Mock both models to return safe/pass verdicts. Verify the post is published to the public feed and is not held in the review queue or silently dropped.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the pipeline exceeds the latency SLA. Mocked models hide real inference latency (the likely bottleneck). To catch this failure in production, the test must run the real vision and text models, as their inference time dominates the latency budget. Without actual model execution, a test suite will pass while production fails. Network calls to external models (if any) add to this; some teams might classify this as System-level if models are external APIs, but the core bottleneck is model inference itself.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post through the full moderation pipeline using actual vision and text models. Measure end-to-end latency from submission to publish decision. Verify it completes within 5 seconds under normal load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: when the vision model is down, images are auto-approved or silently dropped instead of queued. The core failure is in error handling and queue integration, not model availability itself. We can mock the vision model to raise a connection error and test that the error path correctly enqueues the post. The queue system must be real (not mocked) to catch cases where queueing is broken. No need to test actual model unavailability; simulating the error is sufficient to catch the integration failure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model to raise a connection error. Submit an image post and verify it is enqueued for retry rather than auto-approved or dropped. Confirm the retry queue contains the post with the correct image data and metadata.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the real vision model returns inconsistent classifications for the same borderline image on repeated submissions. This is a property of real model non-determinism (or lack thereof) that cannot be tested with a mock. Mocking always returns the same value and hides cases where the real model's stochasticity or caching behavior causes inconsistency. The test must use the real model to verify consistency or catch non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image to the real vision model multiple times within a short window (e.g., 5 submissions within 10 seconds). Verify the model returns consistent classification results and the moderation decision (flagged vs. published) is reproducible across all submissions.
